description: Spolia Opima (“rich spoils” in Latin) was the highest, rarest military honor in ancient Rome, awarded only when a Roman commander personally killed the enemy leader in hand-to-hand combat. The arms of the vanquished were dedicated to Jupiter Feretrius in a prestigious ceremony, with only three instances historically recognized: by Romulus, Aulus Cornelius Cossus, and Marcus Claudius Marcellus.
tooltip: Marcus Claudius Marcellus +100% attack bonus vs. enemy Heroes.
